POSSIBLE TORNADO TOUCHDOWN IN HAWTHORNE NEVADA. THREE HOMES AND FIVE BUSINESSES SEVERELY DAMAGED. REPORTED BY MINERAL COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE.
TORNADO TOUCHDOWN IN HAWTHORNE NEVADA. SEVERAL HOMES AND FIVE BUSINESSES SEVERELY DAMAGED. REPORTED BY MINERAL COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE.
A CONFIRMED TORNADO WITH A RATING OF EF-1 MOVED THROUGH HAWTHORNE THIS AFTERNOON. THE DAMAGE SWATH WAS 30-40 YARDS WIDE FOR ABOUT 1.85 MILES.
THE RECORD COURIER POSTED A VIDEO ONLINE OF FLASH FLOODING AT EAST VALLEY AND PINE NUT ROAD IN THE GARDNERVILLE RANCHOS. WATER WAS RUSHING ACROSS THE ROADWAY.
KTVN CHANNEL 2 POSTED A VIDEO OF FLOODING ALONG CAYUSE DRIVE NEAR DRESSLERVILLE. WATER IS RUSHING OVER THE ROAD BUT APPEARS TO BE FOLLOWING A PREVIOUS WASH.
RECEIEVED A PHOTO REPORT ON FACEBOOK OF MUD AND ROCKS ACROSS ALTERNATE HIGHWAY 95 NORTH AT MCLEOD HILL.

LYON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTS HIGHWAY 342 THROUGH SILVER CITY WAS FLOODED WITH GRAVEL, DEBRIS, AND WATER FOR A PERIOD OF TIME THIS EVENING. TRAFFIC WAS DIVERTED ONTO THE ALTERNATE TRUCK ROUTE. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR DATA.

CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED DEBRIS FLOW AND MUD COVERING THE ROADWAY ALONG U.S. HIGHWAY 395 AND BUCKEYE ROAD.

TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED DANGEROUS FLASH FLOODING IS OCCURRING IN THE SIX MILE CANYON AREA. WATER IS FLOWING INTO HOMES AND PROPERTY DAMGAE IS OCCURRING. SPOTTER REPORTED 1.15 INCHES OF RAINFALL IN LESS THAN 30 MINUTES.
MONO COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTING ROCKS IN THE ROADWAY ALONG U.S. HIGHWAY 395 NEAR TOPAZ LAKE ALONG WITH HEAVY RAINFALL STILL OCCURRING IN THE AREA.
DOUGLAS COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE IS REPORTING FLASH FLOODING IS OCCURRING IN VARIOUS PLACES ALONG FOOTHILL ROAD. ROADS ARE IMPASSABLE AND LAST REPORT WAS FLOODING WAS GETTING WORSE.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED FLASH FLOODING IS OCCURRING IN THE NORTHWEST RENO AREA. VERY HEAVY RAINFALL IS STILL OCCURRING.
LYON COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTING A VEHICLE IS BEING PULLED OUT OF A FLASH FLOOD IN THE SIX MILE CANYON AREA.
VIDEO OF FLOODING AT THE INTERSECTION OF PLUMB AND ARLINGTON. ROADS ARE CURRENTLY CLOSED.

TREE DAMAGE AT EVANS AND 5TH STREET NEAR CITY BUS TERMINAL IN RENO. TREES WERE 20 TO 30 FEET TALL AND LIMBS THAT WERE BROKEN OFF RANGED FROM 1 INCH TO 6 INCHES DIAMETER. MOST LIMBS WERE 2 TO 3 INCHES IN DIAMETER THAT WERE BROKEN OFF.
